problem: Mark and Sandy are walking to the convenience store at the midpoint of their coordinates. Mark is standing at $(0,7)$, and Sandy is standing at $(-4,-1)$. At what coordinate will they meet?

classified_questions.question: Can you describe what the midpoint of two coordinates means in this scenario?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How can we determine the midpoint between two coordinates?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: If Mark is standing at $(0, 7)$ and Sandy is standing at $(-4, -1)$, what is the x-coordinate of the midpoint?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: And what is the y-coordinate of the midpoint?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: By combining the x and y-coordinates, can you determine the final coordinate where Mark and Sandy will meet?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you think of any real-life scenarios where finding the midpoint of two coordinates is useful or applicable?
classified_questions.label: e
